qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated path traversal in image404 handlers enables arbitrary file read and write outside the web root
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in view/img/image404Raw.php and view/img/image404.php. The advisory describes SQL injection in objects/category.php fixCleanTitle() function. These are different vulnerability classes (path traversal vs SQL injection) in different files, different attack surfaces, and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described SQL injection vulnerability in objects/category.php fixCleanTitle() function. The only qualifying finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in view/img/image404Raw.php and view/img/image404.php, which is a different vulnerability class (path traversal vs SQL injection) in different files with a different root cause (unsanitized file path construction vs unparameterized SQL query interpolation).
evidence: Advisory describes SQL injection in objects/category.php fixCleanTitle() function with unparameterized clean_title and id variables.
Finding 'path-traversal-image404-arbitrary-file-access' describes path traversal in view/img/image404Raw.php and view/img/image404.php - different vulnerability class and code location.
The root cause mismatch is clear: SQL injection through string interpolation in database queries vs path traversal through filesystem path construction.
